response.justification: Bogdan brings solid full-stack engineering fundamentals accumulated over 15+ years and demonstrates current engagement with agentic tooling and MCP infrastructure at re:cinq, but falls significantly short of the role's core requirements. The position demands 8+ years of production AI solution delivery; his AI-specific work began only in April 2025. Python—a primary required language—is absent from his listed skills. Critical GenAI competencies including vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, and multi-agent frameworks are entirely unmentioned. Cloud experience is narrow, limited to AWS CDK usage in a pricing-team context at Optilyz rather than AI system design. His engineering depth is genuine, but the GenAI leadership expertise this staff-level role requires is not evidenced.
strengths: Long-tenured TypeScript/Node.js engineer with demonstrated staff-level scope and customer-facing technical contributions across multiple organizations
Direct hands-on work with MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq, showing live alignment with the role's technology space
High-throughput data integration background (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby) indicating scalable backend and pipeline fundamentals
concerns: AI/GenAI work began approximately April 2025, totaling roughly 13 months—far below the 8-year production AI delivery minimum the role specifies
Python is absent from the skill set despite being a primary required language; the listed stack is TypeScript/Node.js-centric
No evidence of vector database usage, RAG architecture design, LLM performance optimization, or named multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI

key_factors.factor: Missing Python and core GenAI stack
Active MCP and agentic workflow exposure
